description: You are a science communicator at NASA JPL preparing educational materials for the Perseverance rover landing anniversary. Simulate the night sky as seen from the rover's location at Jezero Crater, Mars (18.44°N, 77.45°E, Altitude -2500m) on February 18, 2023, at 20:00 UTC. Configure the display for clear Martian sky viewing by disabling the atmosphere and ground/landscape, and enabling constellation lines, constellation labels, and planet labels. Capture screenshots of Earth, Saturn, and a wide-field view of the overall Martian sky to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Finally, create an outreach planning file at /home/ga/Desktop/mars_sky_notes.txt containing the observer location, the date, and brief notes about the visibility of Earth and Saturn.
